description: Governed engineering debt register. Each entry records a known structural or tooling debt with explicit scope, risk, next action, and non-goals. Entries are not TODOs — they carry boundaries on what must and must not be done when the debt is resolved.

debts.title: API write capability gate
debts.current_state: Operation-level route taxonomy distinguishes read, state_changing, and validation_only routes, but state_changing routes do not yet require an explicit local operator or write capability dependency.
debts.desired_state: State-changing operations require an explicit local non-network WriteCapabilityDependency while preserving read-only GET access.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Future state-changing endpoints may be added without a consistent operator boundary. Changing the binding host or adding a reverse proxy would expose unauthenticated write endpoints.
debts.next_action: Design minimal LocalOperatorContext and add route-level write dependency with tests.

debts.title: Paper validation capability boundary
debts.current_state: Paper validation exposes order-shaped, execution-shaped, and account-shaped write endpoints under routes_paper_validation.py, protected only by non-claims and paper field semantics.
debts.desired_state: Paper validation routes sit under a distinct prefix, carry a standalone policy check and threat model, and are guarded by an explicit cannot-graduate-to-live gate test.
debts.risk_if_ignored: The object shapes (ticket, fill, account, execution application) are isomorphic to live trading constructs. Without an explicit boundary, future contributors may treat paper validation as a scaffold for live broker adapters.
debts.next_action: Add distinct router prefix, standalone policy check, threat model entry, and graduate-to-live gate test.

debts.title: Receipt-backed write registry
introduced_by: StateCore write paths for proposals, action intents, paper validation, authority grants
debts.current_state: 32 receipt-related functions across 14 statecore files follow the same DB-write + receipt-file-write + ReceiptIndex-upsert pattern without a shared base or registry.
debts.desired_state: A unified ReceiptBackedCommand registry that registers each bounded context's write path, stale guard, and receipt payload, so pattern drift and stale receipt logic duplication are detectable.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Each new bounded context copies the receipt write pattern, introducing subtle drift in stale guard, hash binding, and idempotency behavior.
debts.next_action: Register all existing receipt write paths in a machine-checkable inventory. Decide whether to extract a shared ReceiptBackedCommand base or keep the pattern registered without abstraction.

debts.title: Taskfile check layering
introduced_by: Incremental additions to task check during data-trust and paper-validation slices
debts.current_state: task check runs lint + typecheck + test + integration + frontend + governance + rules + experiments + eval:smoke in a single flat sequence. Experiments and eval:smoke introduce research dependencies and external tooling risk into the standard quality gate.
debts.desired_state: task check is layered into check:fast (lint, typecheck, unit tests), check:ci (integration, frontend, governance, rules), and check:research (experiments, eval:smoke), with task check aliased to check:fast for daily development.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Standard check becomes slower and more brittle as experiments, eval prompts, or new research integrations are added. Contributors learn to skip task check rather than fix unrelated failures.
debts.next_action: Create check:fast, check:ci, check:research targets. Move experiments and eval:smoke to check:research. Keep task check as an alias for check:fast.
non_goals: modifying CI workflow files in this debt entry

debts.title: Dependency grouping and dead dependency review
introduced_by: Mature-wheel policy accumulation across research, agent, eval, and paper slices
debts.current_state: pyproject.toml main dependencies list includes backtrader, vectorbt, ta-lib, pandas-ta, plotly, and openai-agents which have no src-level imports. Research packages (riskfolio-lib, deepeval), agent packages (langgraph), and eval packages share the same dependency group with core runtime packages.
debts.desired_state: Dependencies are grouped into core, data, research, agent, eval, paper, and security groups. Dead dependencies are removed. Taskfile targets use uv sync --group for scoped installs.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Install time, CI time, vulnerability surface, and version conflict surface grow with every slice. Contributors cannot tell which packages are runtime-critical vs research-optional.
debts.next_action: Audit every main dependency for src-level imports. Remove dead dependencies. Create dependency groups in pyproject.toml.
non_goals: changing runtime behavior of any existing code
modifying uv.lock beyond dependency group changes

debts.title: StateCore model split
introduced_by: Incremental addition of personal finance, review, authority, action intent, trade plan, and paper validation models into models.py
debts.current_state: models.py is one 1798-line file containing 26 SQLModel tables across six bounded contexts: personal_finance, review_proposal, authority, action_intent, trade_plan, paper_validation.
debts.desired_state: Models are split by bounded context into separate modules under statecore/, with models.py providing backwards-compatible re-exports.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Continued growth makes migration authorship, OpenAPI schema review, and test fixture isolation harder. Domain ownership boundaries blur as more tables are added to the same file.
debts.next_action: Split personal_finance_models (9 tables, no foreign keys, no governance dependencies) as the first bounded context extraction. Verify SQLModel.metadata registration, OpenAPI schema stability, and migration compatibility.
non_goals: splitting review_proposal or action_intent contexts (high coupling)
changing import paths for external consumers

debts.title: Frontend ReviewActionShell and module split
introduced_by: Incremental cockpit tab additions up to 8 tabs in a single-page vanilla JS client
debts.current_state: app.js is one 1450-line file maintaining global state, selectors, fetch wrapper, renderers, and forms. Three write forms (attestation, scaffold revision, review event) each independently implement attester+reason collection and window.confirm guards.
debts.desired_state: Write forms are unified under a ReviewActionShell pattern. app.js is split into api.js, state.js, and per-view modules. The split preserves the existing vanilla JS + jsdom test compatibility.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Adding Research Workspace, Scenario, Paper Review, or Agent Tasks tabs as additional render paths in the same file will make app.js unmaintainable. Duplicated form patterns create drift in governance UX.
debts.next_action: Extract ReviewActionShell for the three existing write forms. Split api.js and state.js as the first module extraction without changing view structure.
non_goals: migrating to React, Vue, or any framework

debts.title: Toolchain Node/Rust policy
introduced_by: mise.toml pins Node 26.2.0; CI security.yml uses Node 22 and installs Rust stable toolchain
debts.current_state: Local dev (mise.toml) pins Node 26.2.0. CI workflows use Node 22 via actions/setup-node and install Rust stable via dtolnay/rust-toolchain despite the Rust crate being archived (2026-06-13). No Python dependency requires Rust compilation.
debts.desired_state: Node version is unified to a single agreed version across mise.toml and CI. Rust toolchain is removed from CI or documented with a comment explaining which dependency requires it. Version policy is recorded in docs/reference/.
debts.risk_if_ignored: Local vs CI Node version drift causes false-pass local tests or false-fail CI. Rust toolchain installation wastes CI time and confuses maintainers about toolchain requirements.
debts.next_action: Decide Node version target. Remove Rust toolchain from security.yml CI workflow or document the genuine dependency. Add toolchain version policy to docs/reference/.
non_goals: changing Python, uv, pnpm, or task versions
